zotero.abstractNote: Bayesian deep learning is a powerful framework for uncertainty estimation and model selection. However, it is often perceived as computationally expensive and difficult to implement. We present Laplace, a library for the Laplace approximation in deep learning. It provides a simple, modular, and efficient interface to transform pre-trained neural networks into Bayesian models. Laplace is compatible with standard PyTorch workflows and supports various approximations of the Hessian, including Kronecker-factored, diagonal, and full-matrix variants. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on various tasks, including uncertainty estimation, model selection, and continual learning.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 34 (NeurIPS 2021)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 34 (NeurIPS 2021)
